name: Beer Can Chicken
recipeIngredient: Mesquite chips (soaked in water, up to 1 hour)
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove any fat lumps from the cavity of the chicken.
2. Wash the chicken well and blot dry.
3. Combine the seasoning salt, garlic powder, salt, and pepper to taste. Season the bird inside and out.
4. If using Cajun Injector, load the syring and inject 1 tablespoon of sauce into each breast, leg, and thigh.
5. Remove the tab from a beer can. Using a "church key" opening, make 6-7 holes in the top of the can.
6. Spoon any remaining dry rub through the holes.
7. Stand the chicken upright and insert the beer can into the cavity. Spread out the legs to form a tripod with the beer can so the chicken stands upright.
8. Place the chicken in a smoker or grill set up as a smoker.
9. Smoke-cook the chicken for 2 1/2 to 3 hours, or until it is fall-off-the-bone tender.
10. Serve the chicken upright on the beer can on a platter.
NOT: Cajun Injector is a bottled basting mixture.
CRISPY CRITTERS BASTING SAUCE: Combine all ingredients with 1 cup of water in a heavy saucepan and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to a simmer and cook for 5 minutes. This offbeat recipe makes the most succulent barbequed chicken I've ever tasted. The secret: The open can of beer inserted into the cavity. Besides being incredibly tender, the bird makes a great conversation piece. The recipe comes from the Bryce Boar Blazers of Texas. Steven Raichlin, author Miami Spice. John Lane
